IMG_6286.jpg: The first photo I took on the bridge, before I knew I wanted to start this project.
IMG_6823.jpg:
At the start of 2026, I decided to start this photography project.
On this day, the temperature was mild after a deep freeze the week prior, so the ice was half melted into this particularly unique texture.
IMG_6836.jpg: The river surface was glassy and close to melting after few days of mild temperatures.
IMG_6875.jpg: On the left, the river surface was covered in ice sheets showing cracks; on the right, the surface had been water during the day, and the thinnest layer of matte frost appeared at night.
IMG_6977.jpg: Superbowl weekend, Seahawks vs Patriots. The Prudential Tower cheered the team on. (They lost.)
IMG_6984.jpg: Footsteps on the ice! I would not be so brave.
IMG_6982.jpg: A peaceful night of fresh snow.
IMG_7018.jpg: A few balmy days and the river wanted to melt again.
IMG_7112.jpg: Over the next four days, the ice melted for good for the season.
IMG_7113.jpg: As the ice turned into slush, the river stopped reflecting light. The night looked ominous.
IMG_7256.jpg: I took this photo walking to work. It was the first day when the weather felt like spring.
IMG_7278.jpg: A completely still night. A goose makes an appearance.
IMG_7351.jpg: I like the way the water ripples distort the reflection of the prudential center on the right.
IMG_7469.jpg: An eerily calm night with high contrast.
IMG_7545.jpg: Look closely: a sailboat tipped over in front of the Hancock tower. Help is on site.
IMG_7739.jpg: It was raining in this photo, and the fog was atmospheric. To the right of the photo in the distance was a thunderstorm. The horizon was illuminated white by the storm and lightning.
IMG_7744.jpg: I'm not a morning person, so this 6am photo is a rarity. The soft morning light makes this photo one of my favourites in the whole collection.
